title: Lophophytum pyramidale
taxonomy: Lophophytum pyramidale belongs to the plant family Orchidaceae. It is a type of epiphytic orchid native to Madagascar. The species is part of the subtribe Angraecinae and is characterized by its unique, pyramidal growth habit. Within the Orchidaceae family, Lophophytum pyramidale is likely to be classified as a member of the genus Lophophytum. Further specific classification details may be found in botanical databases or scientific publications.

intro: Lophophytum pyramidale, also known as the "Indian pipe cactus" or "ghost orchid," is a rare and epiphytic plant native to the forests of India and Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
